title: 牙医谋杀案
source_file: data\raw\detectiveqa\novel_data_en\203-牙医谋杀案-阿加莎克里斯蒂.txt
summary:
In the waiting room of the dental practice, a soldier named Arlo Bondi is called away by a pageboy. Hercule Poirot observes a man in his thirties enter the lobby, whom Poirot perceives as a dangerous and potentially murderous individual. A pageboy then leads Poirot to a suite on the second floor.

Inside the dental suite, Poirot meets the dentist, Mr. Morley. Despite his usual composure, Poirot experiences intense fear and anxiety regarding the dental procedure, particularly the use of the drill. During the examination, Morley identifies that Poirot requires fillings in two locations and attention for a specific molar. Morley explains that his morning schedule is tense because his assistant, Miss Neville, is away in the countryside tending to a sick relative.

As he works, Morley discusses his upcoming appointment with Alistair Brent, whom he describes as a man of immense, quiet financial power in Britain who lives an unassuming life. Morley also engages Poirot in a conversation regarding democratic ideals and the Belgian monarchy; Poirot corrects Morley, noting that he is Belgian rather than French. Morley mentions his ability to remember faces even when he forgets names, noting a past patient he has yet to identify. After completing the fillings, Morley concludes the appointment. As Poirot departs, he reflects on his earlier impression of the people in the lobby, noting that while they previously all looked like "bad guys," his perspective has changed.
